description: Model sequential uncertain events as a probability tree and compute conditional expected values, conditional variances, and joint probabilities along each branch

fields.name: tree_structure
fields.label: Tree of {node_id, parent_id, event, conditional_probability, payoff, is_terminal}

fields.name: target_conditioning_event
fields.label: Event S used to condition E(X | S)

core_definitions.conditional_probability: P(A | B) = P(AB) / P(B), requires P(B) > 0
core_definitions.joint_probability: P(A and B) = P(A | B) * P(B) = P(B | A) * P(A)
core_definitions.independent_events: P(A and B) = P(A) * P(B) when A, B independent
core_definitions.conditional_expectation: E(X | S) = sum_i[ P(X_i | S) * X_i ]
core_definitions.conditional_variance: Var(X | S) = sum_i[ P(X_i | S) * (X_i - E(X|S))^2 ]

probability_tree_mechanics.structure: Each node represents an event; branches represent conditional probabilities summing to 1 at each node
probability_tree_mechanics.path_probability: Product of conditional probabilities along the path from root to leaf
probability_tree_mechanics.expected_value_of_tree: Sum over all terminal nodes of (path_probability * terminal_payoff)

tree_properties.exhaustive_at_each_node: Branches leaving a node must represent mutually exclusive and exhaustive events
tree_properties.probabilities_sum_to_one: At each branching point, conditional probabilities sum to 1
tree_properties.sequential_independence: Allows compact representation of multi-stage uncertainty

investment_applications.scenario_analysis: Economy → sector → firm earnings tree for EPS forecasting
investment_applications.real_options: Project value under sequential investment decisions
investment_applications.credit_default: Conditional default probability given rating migrations
investment_applications.binomial_option_pricing: Up/down tree of asset price paths

validation.tree_connected: Every non-root node has a valid parent
validation.terminal_nodes_have_payoffs: Every leaf must have a numeric payoff to compute E(X)

compute_joint_probabilities.description: Multiply conditional probabilities along each root-to-leaf path

compute_conditional_expectation.description: E(X | S) along a conditioning branch

tree_malformed.description: Tree does not satisfy structure requirements

errors.message: Tree is malformed — disconnected nodes, probabilities not summing to 1, or missing terminal payoffs

anti_patterns.rule: Do not confuse P(A | B) with P(B | A)
anti_patterns.why: The prosecutor's fallacy — swapping conditional directions is one of the most common probability errors; they are equal only if P(A) = P(B)

anti_patterns.rule: Do not multiply marginal probabilities when events are dependent
anti_patterns.why: P(A and B) = P(A) * P(B) holds only under independence; using it for dependent events overstates or understates joint likelihood

anti_patterns.rule: Do not omit branches that carry zero probability
anti_patterns.why: Explicit zero-probability branches document the analysis and prevent silent tree malformations; removal makes the tree harder to audit

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use a single-scenario 'most likely' outcome instead of full tree expectation
anti_patterns.why: Most-likely path ignores tail scenarios that drive portfolio risk; always compute full E(X) and path-weighted outcomes

goals.description: Model sequential uncertain events as a probability tree and compute conditional expected values, conditional variances, and joint probabilities along each branch

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details
